name: Surround the Garcia de Sola Reservoir — circular via Herrera del Duque
name_original: Rodea el Embalse de Garcia de Sola — circular por Herrera del Duque
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Embalse de Orellana traverse a diverse landscape characterized by a vast man-made reservoir, often called Extremadura's "inland sea." The region features varied terrains, from well-paved surfaces winding through dehesas and woodlands to routes offering views of the Sierra de Pela's rugged quartzite cliffs. Cyclists can expect a mix of flat sections along the reservoir's "Costa Dulce" and challenging climbs into the surrounding hills. This area, situated on the Guadiana River, provides a rich tapestry of scenery for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Embalse de Orellana The most popular road cycling route is Orellana la Vieja and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer Loop, a 74.8 miles (120.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes a significant climb to the Castle of Puebla de Alcocer, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Orellana de la Sierra – Villanueva de la Serena loop from Navalvillar de Pela, a difficult 65.9 miles (106.1 km) path. This route is mostly on well-paved surfaces, connecting towns and offering varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Surround the Garcia de Sola Reservoir — circular via Herrera del Duque, a 44.7 miles (71.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Road cycling around Embalse de Orellana is defined by expansive reservoir views, Mediterranean dehesas, and rolling hills, offering routes for different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de Orellana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Embalse de Orellana?
A: There are over 10 dedicated road cycling routes around Embalse de Orellana, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Embalse de Orellana?
A: The region offers a diverse range of terrains, from well-paved surfaces winding through Mediterranean &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; and woodlands to routes that provide views of the rugged quartzite cliffs of the Sierra de Pela. You'll find a mix of flat sections along the reservoir's 'Costa Dulce' and challenging climbs into the surrounding hills.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Embalse de Orellana features several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992992123/orellana-la-vieja-and-castillo-de-puebla-de-alcocer-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana la Vieja and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 120 km trail that includes a significant climb to the Castle of Puebla de Alcocer, offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana de la Sierra – Villanueva de la Serena loop from Navalvillar de Pela&lt;/a&gt;, a 106 km path mostly on well-paved surfaces.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while cycling?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Embalse de Orellana, often referred to as Extremadura's 'inland sea.' You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Orellana&lt;/a&gt;, Spain's first inland Blue Flag beach, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The climb to the Castle of Puebla de Alcocer also provides exceptional panoramic vistas over the reservoir and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around the reservoirs?
A: Yes, there are excellent circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992993177/surround-the-garcia-de-sola-reservoir-circular-via-herrera-del-duque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Surround the Garcia de Sola Reservoir — circular via Herrera del Duque&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 72 km trail that takes you through varied terrain around the reservoir. While not listed in the guide, the 'Rodeo embalse de orellana' is also known as a 103 km route that circles the Embalse de Orellana itself, offering continuous scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Embalse de Orellana?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling in Embalse de Orellana. During these seasons, the weather is ideal for exploring the extensive routes without the extreme heat of summer.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: While many routes cater to moderate to difficult levels, the 'Costa Dulce' Blue Trail, which guides visitors through cultural points, offers sections that could be suitable for families looking for less strenuous rides. The flat sections along the reservoir's edge also provide easier cycling opportunities.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes?
A: The towns and villages surrounding Embalse de Orellana, such as Orellana la Vieja, Puebla de Alcocer, and Herrera del Duque, offer various amenities including cafes and pubs where cyclists can refuel and rest. Planning your route to pass through these settlements will ensure access to refreshments.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Embalse de Orellana?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the expansive reservoir views, the unique Mediterranean &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, and the rolling hills that define the region. The variety of routes, catering to different ability levels, is also a highlight, allowing many to explore the area's rich tapestry of scenery.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, cycling routes can incorporate cultural and historical elements. For example, the 'Costa Dulce' Blue Trail guides visitors through points of cultural interest like the Orellana Palace Castle and the Church of the Immaculate Conception. The area also features ancient historical sites in nearby towns, including the ruins of the Roman city of Lacimurga.

Q: Where can I park my car to start a road cycling route?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages around Embalse de Orellana, such as Orellana la Vieja, Puebla de Alcocer, and Navalvillar de Pela, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Look for designated parking areas within these communities.
tips: Bridge that crosses the Garcia Sola Reservoir to the north of the swamp. Space with typical Extremadura Dehesas where the water of the swamp breaks the dry surrounding landscape.

Herrera del Duque is a town located in the northeastern tip of the province and is known for its castle that is located on a hill in the town. From the Castle of Herrera del Duque there is a very beautiful panorama of the whole.

The García de Sola Reservoir is an impressive work of engineering located in the Extremadura region, near Talarrubias, in the province of Badajoz. The reservoir is located in an environment of great beauty, surrounded by imposing rock formations and dense vegetation composed of Mediterranean forests, pine forests and eucalyptus trees. It is a place of great value for birdlife, with species such as the griffon vulture, black vulture, black stork, golden eagle and peregrine falcon.
